evals.prompt: I need to add a `maxRetries` field to the Agent CRD in kagent — optional integer, default 3, minimum 1. It should flow through to the agent runtime config so the Python agent can read it. Walk me through the complete workflow.
evals.expected_output: Complete step-by-step workflow covering: (0) check if field exists, (1) edit agent_types.go with correct struct choice + kubebuilder markers, (2) run make controller-manifests, (3) update adk_api_translator.go, (4) update go/api/adk/types.go and python types.py since it flows to Python runtime, (5) regenerate golden files with UPDATE_GOLDEN=true, (6) add E2E test, (7) run tests

assertions.name: mentions_check_existing_first
assertions.description: Recommends checking if the field already exists before writing code

assertions.name: correct_make_command
assertions.description: Uses 'make controller-manifests' (not just 'make -C go generate' + manual cp)

assertions.name: mentions_translator_file
assertions.description: Mentions updating adk_api_translator.go or deployments.go

assertions.name: mentions_python_runtime_step
assertions.description: Mentions updating go/api/adk/types.go and python types.py since field flows to Python runtime

assertions.name: mentions_golden_files
assertions.description: Mentions regenerating golden files with UPDATE_GOLDEN=true

assertions.name: mentions_e2e_test
assertions.description: Mentions adding an E2E test in invoke_api_test.go

assertions.name: correct_struct_choice
assertions.description: Suggests DeclarativeAgentSpec (not a generic AgentSpec) since this is agent runtime config

evals.prompt: My E2E test TestE2EInvokeInlineAgent is timing out with 'context deadline exceeded' in CI but passes locally on my Mac. How do I debug this?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis should mention: KAGENT_LOCAL_HOST detection difference (macOS vs Linux CI), mock LLM server unreachability from Kind pods, how to check pod status and controller logs, CI matrix (sqlite/postgres), the 'fail print info' step in CI logs, and possibly suggest SKIP_CLEANUP=1 for debugging

assertions.name: mentions_kagent_local_host
assertions.description: Mentions KAGENT_LOCAL_HOST as the likely cause of mac-local-passes-but-CI-fails pattern

assertions.name: mentions_mock_llm_reachability
assertions.description: Explains the mock LLM server reachability issue from Kind pods

assertions.name: mentions_pod_status_check
assertions.description: Suggests checking kubectl get pods or kubectl logs

assertions.name: mentions_ci_logs_approach
assertions.description: Mentions how to get CI logs (gh run view or 'fail print info' step)

evals.prompt: The manifests-check CI job is failing on my PR. What happened and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that CRD manifests are out of date, the fix is to run make controller-manifests, review the diff, and commit the generated files

assertions.name: correct_fix_command
assertions.description: Gives 'make controller-manifests' as the fix (not just 'make -C go generate' without the copy step)

assertions.name: mentions_commit_generated_files
assertions.description: Tells user to commit the regenerated files

assertions.name: explains_root_cause
assertions.description: Explains that CRD type changes require regenerating manifests

evals.prompt: I'm reviewing a PR that adds a `logLevel` field to the Agent CRD spec and updates adk_api_translator.go to pass it as an env var to the container. What should I check to make sure the PR is complete and correct?
evals.expected_output: Should check: correct struct placement (DeclarativeAgentSpec), kubebuilder markers (enum validation), codegen files committed (zz_generated.deepcopy.go, CRD YAML, helm templates), golden files regenerated and committed, E2E test added, no Helm template syntax in comments, conventional commit format with -s signing

assertions.name: checks_codegen_committed
assertions.description: Verifies generated files (deepcopy, CRD YAML, helm templates) are committed

assertions.name: checks_golden_files
assertions.description: Checks that golden files are regenerated and committed

assertions.name: checks_e2e_test
assertions.description: Looks for an E2E test for the new field

assertions.name: checks_kubebuilder_markers
assertions.description: Checks kubebuilder validation markers (e.g., enum for log levels)

assertions.name: checks_commit_conventions
assertions.description: Checks conventional commit format and signed commits (-s)

evals.prompt: I need to sync the latest google-adk upstream changes into our fork. What are the highest-risk files I should watch out for, and what specific customizations might break?
evals.expected_output: Should identify _agent_executor.py as highest risk (subclasses A2aAgentExecutor with per-request runner lifecycle, OTel, HITL), _mcp_toolset.py as high risk (subclasses McpToolset), _remote_a2a_tool.py as significant but lower risk (custom replacement, not subclass), converters/ as medium risk (depends on both google-adk event types and a2a-python types), _session_service.py and _memory_service.py as medium risk (extend BaseSessionService/BaseMemoryService). Should note tools/ and models/ are LOW risk since they're additive. Should reference fork-python-adk.md.

assertions.name: identifies_agent_executor_as_high_risk
assertions.description: Identifies _agent_executor.py as the highest-risk file for upstream sync

assertions.name: explains_why_executor_is_risky
assertions.description: Explains that it subclasses upstream A2aAgentExecutor and overrides key methods like _handle_request

assertions.name: mentions_remote_a2a_tool
assertions.description: Mentions _remote_a2a_tool.py as a custom replacement (not a subclass of upstream)

assertions.name: mentions_converters_risk
assertions.description: Mentions converters/ as medium risk due to dependency on both google-adk and a2a-python types

assertions.name: distinguishes_additive_vs_override
assertions.description: Distinguishes between additive customizations (low risk) and overrides/subclasses (high risk)

evals.prompt: What parts of the Go controller layer are custom to our fork vs upstream kagent-dev/kagent? I'm trying to understand the fork boundary before working on a new feature.
evals.expected_output: Should describe the major custom areas: CRDs (v1alpha2 types with memory, context, skills, A2A fields), translator (CRD->K8s resources, 1360 lines, 9 providers), ADK config types (bridge CRDs<->runtime), prompt templates (Go text/template + ConfigMap), database (SQLite/PostgreSQL with sqlc), HTTP server (REST + A2A proxy, 20+ endpoints), OIDC auth, and controllers. Should note ~80% of the controller layer is custom. Should reference fork-go-controller.md.

assertions.name: mentions_crd_types
assertions.description: Identifies go/api/v1alpha2/ CRD types as heavily customized with fork-specific fields

assertions.name: mentions_translator
assertions.description: Identifies the translator layer as core fork divergence with high sync risk

assertions.name: mentions_database_layer
assertions.description: Mentions the database layer (SQLite/PostgreSQL, migrations, sqlc) as custom

assertions.name: mentions_prompt_templates
assertions.description: Mentions prompt template system as fork-specific (Go text/template + ConfigMap includes)

assertions.name: references_fork_reference
assertions.description: Points to fork-go-controller.md reference for detailed breakdown
